description: One of the earliest existing Islamic mosques in China, showcasing the multicultural integration characteristic of Quanzhou.
Ruins of an ancient city gate from the Song and Yuan dynasties, bearing witness to the historical development of Quanzhou's ancient city with high archaeological value.
A popular cultural and creative park blending traditional architecture with modern cultural creativity, a must-visit spot for photography.
A modern coastal leisure area in Quanzhou, ideal for strolling, relaxing, and enjoying sea views.
Well-preserved alleys of traditional Minnan residential houses, reflecting the lifestyle of Quanzhou's ancient city.
The longest ancient beam-style stone bridge in China, renowned as 'No bridge under heaven is longer than this one,' a marvel of ancient engineering.
Ruins of the southern gate of the ancient city from the Song and Yuan dynasties, witnessing the historical changes of Quanzhou.
The most popular folk belief site in Quanzhou, dedicated to Guan Yu and Yue Fei, featuring exquisite architectural carvings.
An ancient walled city from the Ming dynasty, retaining its old city walls and traditional streets and alleys, with a serene and enchanting night view.
